purpose: Apply explicit state machine transitions for workflow entities, reject invalid jumps, and return updated state snapshots that downstream steps can trust.

endpoints.description: Apply a requested event to a workflow state machine and return the updated state.

sidecar_specification.purpose: Explicit workflow state transition engine with invalid-jump protection.

agent_usage.when_to_use: Use when workflow entities move through named states and invalid jumps must be rejected deterministically.
agent_usage.when_not_to_use: Do not use as a generic database layer, queue runner, or notification service.
agent_usage.example_workflow: 1) Load the current state. 2) Call /workflow/state/transition with an event. 3) Persist the returned next_state only when the transition is valid.

common_calls.description: Transition a workflow from validating to pricing

failure_modes.condition: Event does not map to a valid transition from the current state
failure_modes.symptom: 409 error or explicit invalid transition response
failure_modes.recovery: Apply the correct preceding step or repair the workflow state before retrying

forbidden_behaviors.description: Must not write outside declared scope
Must not make external network calls

agent_notes.implementation_hint: Represent valid transitions explicitly as state-event maps, update an in-memory snapshot first, and persist only the sanctioned next state and error metadata.
agent_notes.known_gotchas: Ad hoc state strings drift quickly across code paths, so keep the canonical transition table centralized and test invalid jumps directly.
agent_notes.preferred_approach: Return previous and next state in every success response to simplify audit and reducer logic downstream.
agent_notes.avoid_approach: Do not infer state transitions from side effects and do not silently coerce invalid events into no-ops.
